input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
explanation: The LTL formula captures the requirement that if the sensor limits are exceeded (indicated by 'limits' being true), then the autopilot must engage in a pullup maneuver (indicated by 'pullup') under the condition that the aircraft is not in standby mode (the pilot is not in control) and the system is operational (not in failure mode indicated by 'apfail'). This setup ensures that the autopilot takes corrective action when a fault condition is detected while ensuring safe operation.

input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
explanation: The LTL formula specifies that when the external sensor limits are exceeded (limits is true), the autopilot should initiate a pullup maneuver (pullup is true) if the pilot is not in standby (standby is false) and the system is healthy (supported is true) and not in a failure state (apfail is false). This captures the safety condition regarding the autopilot's operational state.

input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement that when the sensor limits are exceeded, the autopilot should initiate a pullup maneuver, but only under the conditions that the pilot is not in control (not in standby mode), and the system is healthy (not indicating a failure). This logic ensures that the autopilot only acts to pullup when it is safe and appropriate to do so.

input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
explanation: The LTL property expresses that when the pilot is not in control and the system is healthy (not experiencing a failure), if the sensor limits are exceeded, the autopilot should enter a pullup maneuver. input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).

atomic_propositions.standby: True when the pilot is in control of the vehicle
atomic_propositions.apfail: Failure indication from an external source
atomic_propositions.supported: Indication of general health of system, must be True to enable operation
atomic_propositions.limits: External indication to sensor on faulty condition impeding safe operation.
atomic_propositions.pullup: True when the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state

explanation: The LTL formula captures the requirement that when the sensor exceeds its limits (indicated by 'limits'), and the pilot is not in control (not 'standby'), and the system is functioning properly (not 'apfail'), the autopilot should initiate a pullup maneuver (indicated by 'pullup'). This is represented by using a conditional expression to ensure that the autopilot pullup occurs under the specified conditions.

input_natural_language: Exceeding sensor limits shall latch an autopilot pullup when the pilot is not in control (not standby) and the system is supported without failures (not apfail).
explanation: The LTL property ensures that whenever the sensor limits are exceeded, the autopilot will engage a pull-up maneuver only if the pilot is not controlling the vehicle (not in standby) and the system is supported and operational (not in apfail state). This captures the requirement that safety conditions are met before the autopilot can take action.
